DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT BELL SOUND USA LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. In its ruling on the prior demurrer the Court instructed the Plaintiff to allege specific facts (argument, case citations and allegations "on information and belief" without the facts giving rise to such belief are not factual allegations). Plaintiff did not do so. Plaintiff shall have final leave to amend to allege a viable cause of action for interference with contractual relations, including alleging facts (not conclusions) of Defendant's knowledge of the existence and the terms of the contract; facts addressing statute of frauds; and facts addressing fair competition privilege. The Court notes that neither the statute of frauds not the fair competition issues raised in the demurrer were addressed by Plaintiff in opposition. All other causes of action/remedies asserted against this defendant are derivative of the interference with contractual relations. They should only be re-alleged if defendant can in good faith allege a cause of action for interference with contractual relations.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT CBRE, INC., ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CBRE CAPITAL'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. In its ruling on the prior demurrer the Court instructed the Plaintiff to allege specific facts (argument, case citations and allegations "on information and belief" without the facts giving rise to such belief are not factual allegations). Plaintiff did not do so. Plaintiff shall have final leave to amend to allege in good faith all elements of breach of contract with this defendant, i.e. contract formation and breach of specific terms (whether independent contract or "multi-party" contract Plaintiff to allege each element as to this defendant). See Warington Lumber Co. v. Fullerton Mortg. & Escrow Co. (1963) 222 Cal. App. 2d 706. As to the promissory estoppel cause of action, Plaintiff to allege each element of this cause of action as to this Defendant. Plaintiff shall further allege fraud with particularity as to this Defendant. For declaratory relief cause of action, to the extent it may be based on claims to property, Plaintiff to allege this Defendant's interest in the property. As to all other causes of action and/or remedies, they depend on viability of contract-based and fraud causes of action and shall be re-alleged only if those causes of action are properly asserted. All new allegations to be in bold font.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Bell Sound USA LLC Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a viable cause of action for interference with contractual relations, including alleging facts (not conclusions) of Defendant's knowledge of the existence and the terms of the contract; facts addressing statute of frauds; and facts addressing fair competition privilege. The Court notes that neither the statute of frauds not the fair competition issues raised in the demurrer were addressed by Plaintiff in opposition. They should only be re-alleged if defendant can in good faith allege a cause of action for interference with contractual relations. Plaintiff has thirty days from the signing of the order to amend.
